report_name: Estonia Reports 1,100+ Cyber Incidents in April, Driven by Phishing and Service Outages

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Phishing Actor
threat_actors.description: An unidentified actor or set of actors responsible for conducting widespread, financially motivated phishing campaigns in Estonia during April 2026. These campaigns impersonated LHV Bank, MetaMask, and Ledger to steal banking credentials and cryptocurrency private keys.
threat_actors.goals: Financial theft from Estonian citizens through credential and cryptocurrency wallet theft.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers conducted widespread phishing campaigns, sending fraudulent emails that impersonated LHV Bank, MetaMask, and Ledger to trick recipients into visiting malicious websites.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Phishing emails contained malicious links directing victims to fraudulent websites designed to harvest credentials and cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases.

identities.name: LHV Bank
identities.description: An Estonian bank that was impersonated in a widespread phishing campaign designed to steal customer banking credentials.

identities.name: MetaMask
identities.description: A cryptocurrency wallet service whose brand and users were targeted by a phishing campaign to steal private keys and funds.

identities.name: Ledger
identities.description: A cryptocurrency hardware wallet company whose brand and users were targeted by a phishing campaign to steal private keys and funds.

identities.name: Health Insurance Fund
identities.description: An Estonian government agency whose digital prescription system and other services experienced multiple outages due to software errors and external provider failures.

identities.name: DigiDoc4
identities.description: An Estonian digital signature application that suffered an outage, preventing users from providing digital signatures.

identities.name: Information System Authority (RIA)
identities.description: Estonia's national information system authority that documented and reported on the 1,138 cyber incidents in April 2026.

identities.name: CERT-EE
identities.description: Estonia's Computer Emergency Response Team, which identified the targeted attacks against cryptocurrency wallet users.

infrastructure.name: LHV Bank Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Fraudulent websites created to impersonate LHV Bank and harvest user banking credentials.

infrastructure.name: Cryptocurrency Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Fake websites designed to impersonate MetaMask and Ledger wallet services to steal users' private keys or seed phrases.

indicators.name: Potential Typosquatting of Financial Service Domains
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Typosquatting of Financial Service Domains
id: 2b1a3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for domains that may be typosquatted versions of LHV Bank, MetaMask, or Ledger, as seen in April 2026 phishing campaigns against Estonian users.
references:
  - https://www.identityweek.net/disruptions-to-digital-services-and-a-surge-in-phishing-emails/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-09
tags:
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'lhv-'
      - 'metamask-'
      - 'ledger-'
  filter:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.lhv.ee'
      - '.metamask.io'
      - '.ledger.com'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for domains potentially related to phishing campaigns impersonating LHV Bank, MetaMask, and Ledger by looking for common typosquatting patterns.

indicators.name: Suspicious URL Pattern for Credential Phishing
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious URL Pattern for Credential Phishing
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-987654321fed
status: experimental
description: Detects web proxy traffic to URLs containing patterns commonly used in credential phishing pages, such as passing user information in parameters to a login script.
references:
  - https://www.identityweek.net/disruptions-to-digital-services-and-a-surge-in-phishing-emails/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-09
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains|all:
      - '/login'
      - 'user='
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web traffic to URLs with patterns often seen in phishing pages, such as a login script path combined with user information in URL parameters.

relationships.source_name: Potential Typosquatting of Financial Service Domains
Suspicious URL Pattern for Credential Phishing
